text: Mumbai (मुंबई, formerly Bombay) is India's financial capital, Bollywood entertainment hub, and most populous city with 21+ million metro population. Originally seven islands (Colaba, Old Woman's Island, Worli, Mahim, Mazagaon, Parel, Bombay) now connected by causeways/land reclamation. Commercial center western India, contrasts billionaires/slums, skyscrapers/dhovadi boats.

Mumbai tropical wet-dry climate (Köppen Aw), hot/humid year-round. Monsoon Jun-Sep 90% annual 2,300mm rainfall, flooding common. Summers Mar-May 30-40°C high humidity. Pleasant Oct-Feb 20-30°C. High humidity 70-90%, sea breezes provide relief.

subsection: Districts - South Mumbai and Western Suburbs
text: South Mumbai (Colaba, Fort, Marine Lines, Cuffe Parade, Malabar Hill) – colonial architecture, business district, luxury hotels. Western Suburbs (Bandra, Khar, Santa Cruz, Juhu, Andheri, Versova, Goregaon) – Bollywood studios, trendy cafes, beaches, middle-class residential.

Central Suburbs (Dadar, Mahim, Matunga, Sion, Dharavi) – middle-class, universities, markets. North Mumbai/Navi Mumbai (Vashi, Nerul, Airoli, Ghansoli) – IT parks, modern townships. Thane/Virar/Vasai – extended suburbs, commuter towns.

Airports: Chhatrapati Shivaji (BOM T1 domestic, T2 international), domestic 4km city center. Metro Aqua Line 3 direct airport connection. Kaali Peeli taxis ₹23/km meter from airport; use prepaid booths to avoid scams. Uber/Ola ₹15-25/km also available from terminal.

Ferries operate from Gateway of India: Elephanta Caves ferry ₹200 return, Manori/Gorai beach ferries from Borivali/Bhayander. Ferry services also connect Mumbai to Mandwa (for Alibaug). Main ferry terminal at Bhaucha Dhakka (Ferry Wharf) for passenger vessel services.

Mumbai Local Trains (WR/CR) lifeline, 2nd busiest system world. Peak hours chaotic, ladies compartments, 1st class ₹20-50. BEST buses red (AC/non-AC ₹10-50), crowded. Mumbai Metro Lines 1(blue Versova-Andheri-Ghatkopar), 2(yellow), 3(aqua), Line 1 ₹10-40.

Kaali Peeli taxis ₹23/km meter, Uber/Ola ₹15-25/km. Auto-rickshaws permitted only in suburbs (north of Sion/Mahim); not allowed in South Mumbai. Marine Drive walk/bike best for sightseeing. Churchgate and CST stations are central hubs for onward travel.

Gateway of India 26m yellow basalt arch (1924) Colaba, built for King George V visit. Indo-Saracenic architecture dome, Hindu/Muslim/Portuguese elements. Main departure Elephanta Caves ferries. Taj Mahal Palace Hotel opposite. Free entry, always crowded vendors/touts.

Marine Drive 3km Queen's Necklace promenade Nariman Point-Girgaum Chowpatty. Art Deco apartments curve along Arabian Sea. Iconic dusk skyline, illuminated nights. Walk/bike best. Churchgate/Marine Lines stations. Evening crowds, street food Chowpatty end.

UNESCO Sites: Chhatrapati Shivaji Terminus (Victorian Gothic railway station), Elephanta Caves (7th-century rock-cut Hindu/Buddhist temples). Iconic landmarks: Gateway of India (1924 Indo-Saracenic arch), Marine Drive (Queen's Necklace promenade), Haji Ali Dargah (offshore Sufi shrine), Rajabai Clock Tower.

Religious: Siddhivinayak Temple (Ganesha shrine Prabhadevi), Mahalaxmi Temple, ISKCON Juhu, Mount Mary Church Bandra. Nature: Sanjay Gandhi National Park (urban forest, Kanheri Caves), Juhu Beach, Chowpatty Beach, Hanging Gardens Malabar Hill, Bandstand promenade.

Bollywood studio tours (Filmcity, ND Studios), Mumbai Darshan bus tours, Elephanta Caves ferry (Gateway of India), Dharavi slum tours (Reality Tours), street food walks Colaba/Chandni Chowk style. Marine Drive sunset walks, local train rides (tourist class AC).

Colaba Causeway (touristy clothes/antiques/souvenirs), Crawford Market (wholesale fruit/spices/pets, colonial building), Linking Road Bandra (street fashion ₹200-800), Chor Bazaar (antiques Mutton Street), Fashion Street (budget clothes near CST), High-end malls: Phoenix Palladium Lower Parel, Infiniti Mall Andheri, Palladium Worli.

Mumbai street food: vada pav (potato fritter bun ₹20), pav bhaji (vegetable mash bread ₹50), bhel puri/sev puri Chowpatty, seafood (pomfret, bombil fry). Irani cafes (Yazdani, Britannia), South Indian idli-dosa Matunga, Parsi dhansak Grant Road, fine dining Taj President.

Nightlife hotspots: Bandra (Social, Bonobo), Lower Parel/Worli (Tryst, Asia 7), Colaba (GBB, Cafe Mondegar), Juhu beach shacks (summer only). Rooftop bars: Dome Aerocity, Asilo at St Regis. Mumbai dry days (Gandhi Jayanti, elections). Last call 1:30AM.

Budget: Colaba guesthouses (₹1500-3000), Parsi/Anand Bhavan. Midrange: Bandra/Khar (₹4000-8000), Juhu service apartments. Luxury: Taj Mahal Palace (₹20,000+), Oberoi Trident Nariman Point, JW Marriott Juhu, St Regis Lower Parel. Book 3 months ahead peak season.

Mumbai relatively safe but crowded trains pickpockets, avoid ladies compartment peak hours. Taxi scams airport (use prepaid), touts Gateway of India. Women: modest dress, avoid isolated beaches night. Safe areas: South Mumbai, Bandra.

Monsoon Jun-Sep flooding, potholes, leptospirosis risk from waterlogging. Avoid wading through floodwater. High humidity year-round; carry water and ORS sachets. Stomach issues common from street food; eat at hygienic stalls. Nearest hospitals: KEM Hospital Parel, Lilavati Bandra, Hinduja Mahim, Breach Candy Colaba.
Pune 150km southeast, IT city, Shaniwar Wada, Aga Khan Palace, Osho Ashram. Expressway drive 2.5hrs, Deccan Express train 3.5hrs (₹300 AC Chair), Shivneri bus 3hrs (₹350). Popular day/overnight trip.
Alibaug 100km south, coastal town with Kolaba Fort, Alibaug Beach, Kashid Beach. Ferry from Gateway of India 1hr (₹300), road via NH66 2.5hrs. Weekend beach getaway from Mumbai.

Lonavala 83km east, popular hill station Western Ghats, Bhushi Dam, Karla/Bhaja Caves, tiger point. Train 1.5hrs from CST (₹50-200), drive 1.5hrs Mumbai-Pune Expressway. Best visited monsoon/post-monsoon (Jun-Oct) for waterfalls.
